title: Osteoarthritis follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports usage-related joint pain, stiffness, motion restriction, tenderness, swelling, instability, functional limitation, night pain, fever, redness or heat, weight loss, and systemic concern features.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped OA-compatible symptoms, function, quality-of-life, and assessed joint findings.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports usage-related joint pain, stiffness, motion restriction, tenderness, swelling, instability, functional limitation, night pain, fever, redness or heat, weight loss, and systemic concern features.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28

investigations.text: Defines clinical diagnosis in typical adults, cautions that radiographic findings may not match symptoms, and limits MRI or other imaging to selected uncertainty or alternative-diagnosis contexts.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped OA terminology and clinician-entered X-ray or prior-imaging review without inferring diagnosis.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines clinical diagnosis in typical adults, cautions that radiographic findings may not match symptoms, and limits MRI or other imaging to selected uncertainty or alternative-diagnosis contexts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28

investigations.text: Defines concern and specialist contexts including a hot swollen joint, persistent fever, night symptoms, weight loss, face-to-face assessment needs, worsening symptoms, severe functional limitation, and selected imaging.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped worsening, acute swelling, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines concern and specialist contexts including a hot swollen joint, persistent fever, night symptoms, weight loss, face-to-face assessment needs, worsening symptoms, severe functional limitation, and selected imaging.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28

follow_up.text: Describes knee pain location, stairs-related pain, giving way, instability, and hip groin or thigh pain, stiffness, walking pain, restricted movement, and early loss of internal rotation.
follow_up.rationale: Reviewed to confirm the guideline is strongest for knee and hip osteoarthritis; no multi-joint legacy items were mapped from that section alone.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Describes knee pain location, stairs-related pain, giving way, instability, and hip groin or thigh pain, stiffness, walking pain, restricted movement, and early loss of internal rotation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28

source_limitations: Analgesia use, lifestyle or exercise, weight management, physiotherapy, joint protection, walking aids, activity modification, and fixed three-to-six-month timing remain unsupported by the reviewed exact sections.
Osteoarthritis remains a clinician-stated diagnosis and radiographic findings must not be interpreted automatically.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
